name: pvgafb
transitions: real->protected (CR0.PE 0->1, CS far-jump to 32-bit flat code segment 0x08)
VGA mode-13h chain-4 mode-set: SEQ[2]=0x0F (plane-write all), SEQ[4]=0x0E (CHN_4M bit3), GFX[6]=0x05 (graphics + memory-map mode 1 = 0xA0000 64K), GFX[5]=0x40 (256-colour)
chain-4 LINEAR write/read-back (SR2=0x0F): write 0x11/0x22/0x33/0x44 at 0xA0000..0xA0003 (all four plane lanes), 0x55 at 0xA0010, 0x66 at 0xAF000; read each back -> identical (linear identity map)

per-byte PLANE GATING: establish 0xAA/0xBB/0xCC/0xDD at 0xA0100..0xA0103 with all planes; set SR2=0x05 (planes 0,2 only); overwrite with 0x11/0x22/0x33/0x44; read back -> 0x11/0xBB/0x33/0xDD (planes 1,3 KEEP prior value); restore SR2=0x0F
why_per_record_differential: pvgafb has NO interrupts and every interaction is a synchronous IN/OUT (VGA mode-set) or a chain-4 linear memory write/read. qemu's gdbstub single-step (gen_trace.py --system) is therefore a valid per-record oracle and the gate is plain compare.py EQUIVALENT, running on ventium_soc with the M8.6 ven_vga_fb VRAM. compare.py --mode func grades GPRs per retired instruction, so the movzbl read-backs of written pixels into eax are directly diffable.
determinism_contract: Models ONLY the mode-13h CHAIN-4 LINEAR path: a 64 KiB byte aperture at 0xA0000 where a read of +N returns vram[N] (identity, no plane select) and a write of +N lands in vram[N] iff the SR2 plane-write bit (N&3) is set (qemu vga.c chain-4). The test sets chain-4 mode FIRST and never accesses 0xA0000 before (the RTL gates its VRAM intercept on the same chain-4-enable bits, so a pre-chain-4 access falls through to backing RAM — it never happens here). EXCLUDED from the differential (oracle/board boundary): scan-out / pixel rendering / DAC palette expansion to RGB / refresh (the PS A53 owns scan-out on the board), the VGA planar/latched write-modes 0-3 + read-mode 1 + rotate/bit-mask/set-reset/odd-even/compare, and the 0xB0000/0xB8000 memory-map windows.
comparison_plan: verif/soc/run-soc-vgafb-gate.sh: builds pvgafb.bin + the ventium_soc --soc TB, confirms the image reaches isa-debug-exit (133) under qemu-system-i386, regenerates the per-record golden via gen_trace.py --system (drift-checked vs the committed pvgafb.sys.vtrace.golden), runs ventium_soc on the image, and diffs the two streams with compare.py --mode func -> EQUIVALENT over all retired instructions. EQUIVALENT = the CPU-visible mode-13h chain-4 framebuffer behaviour is byte-identical to qemu-system-i386 8.2.2.
description: Bare-metal M8.6 VGA mode-13h chain-4 framebuffer differential (NO paging, NO interrupts). Boots real->protected, puts the VGA into chain-4 linear mode (SEQ[2]/SEQ[4]/GFX[5]/GFX[6]), then exercises the 64 KiB CPU-visible VRAM at 0xA0000 that M8.6 adds to ventium_soc (ven_vga_fb): chain-4 linear write/read-back identity at several offsets, and per-byte plane gating where SR2 != 0x0F keeps the masked lanes at their prior value (the behaviour a plain-RAM 0xA0000 gets WRONG). Graded PER-RECORD against the qemu-system-i386 single-step golden. Scan-out / palette / planar modes are documented oracle boundaries (the PS A53 owns scan-out). This is the CPU-visible pixel-memory piece on the path to the first Quake frame (F4).
